content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding strip_metadata keyword-only param to exif_transpose() in ImageOps.py (function spans lines ~676-741, includes @overload signatures). When True, after transpose, remove all metadata (exif, info dict entries) instead of just orientation.

fields.note: Adding strip_metadata keyword-only param to exif_transpose() in ImageOps.py (function spans lines ~676-741, includes @overload signatures). When True, after transpose, remove all metadata (exif, info dict entries) instead of just orientation.

Proposing: add both transform_callback and strip_metadata as new keyword-only params. Restructured tail so exif_image is always defined, then strip_metadata is applied uniformly once, avoiding duplicated branches. Also need to add Callable to the collections.abc import line. Let me know if your transform_callback signature/name differs from what I guessed.

Two fixes to your draft: (1) used Image.Transpose consistently instead of bare Transpose, since no such alias is imported anywhere in this file - adding one would be an unrelated import change; (2) replaced the old elif not in_place: return image.copy() / return None tail with an else branch that still applies strip_metadata when there are no methods to apply (e.g. orientation=1, or callback returns None/empty) - otherwise strip_metadata silently does nothing when no transpose is needed, which is a real gap for the metadata-stripping use case. Also need collections.abc Callable import (Sequence already imported). Please confirm or send counter-DRAFT.

Agree with everything except the overloads: restored transform_callback and strip_metadata onto both @overload signatures (not just the impl signature), otherwise mypy rejects e.g. exif_transpose(img, in_place=True, strip_metadata=True) as an unexpected keyword against both overloads. Everything else - your methods-list loop, the elif/else exif_image assignment, and the single clear()-based strip_metadata block - kept verbatim from your last message. If you agree, please send AGREE with this exact code so we both implement identically.
Confirmed - echoing your exact code back so we both write byte-identical text. Will implement this verbatim, plus the import line change to: from collections.abc import Callable, Sequence
